summary: CLI silently relays user prompts through author-controlled LLM accounts
details: The `buddyme` CLI reads user queries via `input("query: ")` in buddyMe/main.py and dispatches them to an Agent that calls third-party LLM endpoints (open.bigmodel.cn, api.deepseek.com, qianfan.baidubce.com, dashscope.aliyuncs.com, api.xiaomimimo.com) using API keys hardcoded in buddyMe/llm_moudle/model_config.py. Every prompt the user submits is therefore routed through the author's vendor accounts, with no disclosure, configuration option, or opt-out, giving the author visibility into all caller-supplied prompts via each provider's dashboard. The same module ships live keys for GLM, DeepSeek, Baidu Qianfan ERNIE, Xiaomi MiMo, and Qwen; baidu_search_tool.py duplicates the Baidu key. The hardcoded credentials are the author's own (author-self-harm component), but the silent-relay of caller-supplied prompt data through the package's documented API to author-controlled accounts is the installer-impacting issue.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
